𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄

THE ANCIENT COVENANT RECORD

Volume Nineteen — Part Two

The Book of Yehezkel

YEHEZKEL

The Book of Ezekiel — 4QEzeka · 4QEzekb · 11QEzek Primary

Chapters 12 through 19

The Sign of the Exile — False Prophets — The Foundling Wife — Individual Accountability — Lamentation for the Princes

PRIMARY MANUSCRIPT AUTHORITIES

4QEzeka · 4QEzekb · 11QEzek — Dead Sea Scrolls — Cave 4 and Cave 11 Qumran

Ethiopian Orit Ge'ez — Complete Canon

YHWH and The Creator only

COMPARATIVE NOTES AT END OF EVERY CHAPTER

DSS · ORIT GE'EZ · MASORETIC VARIANT · CRITICAL NOTE

YHWH / 𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄 and The Creator only — no substitute titles

All personal names in original Hebrew form throughout

YEHEZKEL — CHAPTERS 12 THROUGH 19 — SIGN ACTS ALLEGORIES AND COVENANT ACCOUNTABILITY

YEHEZKEL — CHAPTER 12 The Sign of the Exile — Dig Through the Wall — The Prince Shall Bear His Baggage — The Days Are at Hand

1 The word of YHWH came to me:

2 Son of man, you dwell in the midst of a rebellious house, who have eyes to see, but see not, who have ears to hear, but hear not, for they are a rebellious house.

3 As for you, son of man, prepare for yourself an exile's baggage, and go into exile by day in their sight. You shall go like an exile from your place to another place in their sight. Perhaps they will understand, though they are a rebellious house.

4 You shall bring out your baggage by day in their sight, as baggage for exile. And you shall go out yourself at evening in their sight, as those do who go into exile.

5 In their sight dig through the wall, and bring your baggage out through it.

6 In their sight you shall lift the baggage upon your shoulder and carry it out at dusk. You shall cover your face so that you cannot see the land, for I have made you a sign for the house of Yisra'EL.

7 And I did as I was commanded. I brought out my baggage by day, as baggage for exile, and in the evening I dug through the wall with my own hands. I brought it out at dusk, carrying it on my shoulder in their sight.

8 In the morning the word of YHWH came to me:

9 Son of man, has not the house of Yisra'EL, the rebellious house, said to you, What are you doing?

10 Say to them, Thus says the Creator YHWH: This oracle concerns the prince in Yerushalayim and all the house of Yisra'EL who are in it.

11 Say, I am a sign for you: as I have done, so shall it be done to them. They shall go into exile, into captivity.

12 And the prince who is among them shall lift his baggage upon his shoulder at dusk, and shall go out. They shall dig through the wall to bring him out through it. He shall cover his face, that he may not see the land with his eyes.

13 And I will spread my net over him, and he shall be taken in my snare. And I will bring him to Babylon, the land of the Chaldeans, yet he shall not see it, and he shall die there.

14 And I will scatter toward every wind all who are around him, his helpers and all his troops, and I will unsheathe the sword after them.

15 And they shall know that I am YHWH, when I disperse them among the nations and scatter them among the countries.

16 But I will let a few of them escape from the sword, from famine and pestilence, that they may declare all their abominations among the nations where they go, and they shall know that I am YHWH.

17 And the word of YHWH came to me:

18 Son of man, eat your bread with quaking, and drink water with trembling and with anxiety.

19 And say to the people of the land, Thus says the Creator YHWH concerning the inhabitants of Yerushalayim in the land of Yisra'EL: They shall eat their bread with anxiety, and drink water in dismay, because their land will be stripped of all it contains, on account of the violence of all those who dwell in it.

20 And the inhabited cities shall be laid waste, and the land shall become a desolation, and you shall know that I am YHWH.

21 And the word of YHWH came to me:

22 Son of man, what is this proverb that you have about the land of Yisra'EL, saying, The days grow long, and every vision comes to nothing?

23 Tell them therefore, Thus says the Creator YHWH: I will put an end to this proverb, and they shall use it no more as a proverb in Yisra'EL. But say to them, The days are near, and the fulfillment of every vision.

24 For there shall be no more any false vision or flattering divination within the house of Yisra'EL.

25 For I am YHWH. I will speak the word that I will speak, and it will be performed. It will no longer be delayed, but in your days, O rebellious house, I will speak the word and perform it, declares the Creator YHWH.

26 And the word of YHWH came to me:

27 Son of man, behold, the house of Yisra'EL says, The vision that he sees is for many days from now, and he prophesies of times far off.

28 Therefore say to them, Thus says the Creator YHWH: None of my words will be delayed any longer, but the word that I speak will be performed, declares the Creator YHWH.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The DSS preserves Chapter 12 in close agreement with the Masoretic. The exile sign-act — Yehezkel packing baggage and digging through the wall — is the covenant enactment of Tzidkiyahu's flight from Yerushalayim. Verse 13 — he shall not see it, and he shall die there — is the precise fulfillment of both Yirmeyahu's covenant word and Yehezkel's vision: Tzidkiyahu is blinded before reaching Babylon.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 12 in full. Verse 22-23 — the proverb that every vision comes to nothing is directly countered by YHWH: the days are near, not far. The covenant community in exile had developed a theological defense mechanism: prophetic visions apply to a distant future. YHWH dismantles this covenant delay theology with precision.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 12. Verse 6 — cover your face so that you cannot see the land. The sign-act anticipates the blinding of Tzidkiyahu — he will be carried to Babylon having seen the king of Babylon face to face — Yirmeyahu 34:3 — and then blinded before arrival, fulfilling both prophets simultaneously.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 28 — none of my words will be delayed any longer, but the word that I speak will be performed. The covenant declaration against prophetic deferral: the covenant community had learned to neutralize the covenant word by projecting its fulfillment into the indefinite future. YHWH's response is the collapse of the temporal buffer: the word is for now, not for later. The covenant judgment is imminent, not theoretical.

YEHEZKEL — CHAPTER 13 Woe to the Foolish Prophets — They Have Plastered the Wall With Whitewash — The Prophets Who See False Visions — Women Who Sew Magic Bands

1 The word of YHWH came to me:

2 Son of man, prophesy against the prophets of Yisra'EL, who are prophesying, and say to those who prophesy from their own hearts: Hear the word of YHWH.

3 Thus says the Creator YHWH, Woe to the foolish prophets who follow their own spirit, and have seen nothing.

4 Your prophets have been like jackals among ruins, O Yisra'EL.

5 You have not gone up into the breaches, or built up a wall for the house of Yisra'EL, that it might stand in battle on the day of YHWH.

6 They have seen false visions and lying divinations. They say, Declares YHWH, when YHWH has not sent them, and yet they expect him to fulfill their word.

7 Have you not seen a false vision and uttered a lying divination, whenever you have said, Declares YHWH, although I have not spoken?

8 Therefore thus says the Creator YHWH: Because you have uttered falsehood and seen lying visions, therefore behold, I am against you, declares the Creator YHWH.

9 My hand will be against the prophets who see false visions and who give lying divinations. They shall not be in the council of my people, nor be enrolled in the register of the house of Yisra'EL, nor shall they enter the land of Yisra'EL. And you shall know that I am the Creator YHWH.

10 Precisely because they have misled my people, saying, Peace, when there is no peace, and because, when the people build a wall, these prophets smear it with whitewash,

11 Say to those who smear it with whitewash that it shall fall. There will come a deluge of rain, and you, O great hailstones, will fall, and a stormy wind break out.

12 And when the wall falls, will it not be said to you, Where is the coating with which you smeared it?

13 Therefore thus says the Creator YHWH: I will make a stormy wind break out in my wrath, and there shall be a deluge of rain in my anger, and great hailstones in wrath to make a full end.

14 And I will break down the wall that you have smeared with whitewash, and bring it down to the ground, so that its foundation is laid bare. When it falls, you shall perish in the midst of it, and you shall know that I am YHWH.

15 Thus will I spend my wrath upon the wall and upon those who have smeared it with whitewash, and I will say to you, The wall is no more, nor those who smeared it,

16 The prophets of Yisra'EL who prophesied concerning Yerushalayim and saw visions of peace for her, when there was no peace, declares the Creator YHWH.

17 And you, son of man, set your face against the daughters of your people, who prophesy out of their own minds. Prophesy against them

18 And say, Thus says the Creator YHWH: Woe to the women who sew magic bands upon all wrists, and make veils for the heads of persons of every stature, in the hunt for souls. Will you hunt down souls belonging to my people and keep your own souls alive?

19 You have profaned me among my people for handfuls of barley and for pieces of bread, putting to death souls who should not die and keeping alive souls who should not live, by your lying to my people, who listen to lies.

20 Therefore thus says the Creator YHWH: Behold, I am against your magic bands with which you hunt the souls like birds, and I will tear them from your arms, and I will let the souls whom you hunt go free, the souls like birds.

21 Your veils also I will tear off and deliver my people out of your hand, and they shall be no more in your hand as prey, and you shall know that I am YHWH.

22 Because you have disheartened the righteous falsely, although I have not grieved him, and you have encouraged the wicked, that he should not turn from his evil way to save his life,

23 Therefore you shall no more see false visions or practice divination. I will deliver my people out of your hand. And you shall know that I am YHWH.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The DSS preserves Chapter 13 in close agreement with the Masoretic. The whitewashed wall — verses 10-16 — is the covenant metaphor for false prophetic ministry: the prophets cover the structural defects of the covenant community with a coating of false peace. The wall still collapses — and the whitewash makes it worse by concealing the danger until the moment of catastrophic failure.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 13 in full. Verses 17-23 address female prophets who practice magic for profit — hunting souls for handfuls of barley and pieces of bread. The covenant condemnation is not of female prophecy as such but of the commercialized magical practice that puts to death souls who should not die and keeps alive those who should not live.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 13. Verse 5 — you have not gone up into the breaches or built up a wall for the house of Yisra'EL. The covenant failure of the false prophets is their absence at the covenant breach: when the covenant community needed genuine covenant confrontation, the false prophets were absent from the breach — standing behind their whitewashed walls, not in the gap.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 6 — they say, Declares YHWH, when YHWH has not sent them, and yet they expect him to fulfill their word. The covenant presumption of the false prophet: appropriating the covenant formula — declares YHWH — and then expecting covenant reality to conform to the falsely attributed word. The covenant claim produces the covenant expectation of fulfillment. When fulfillment does not come, the covenant formula is discredited along with the false prophet.

YEHEZKEL — CHAPTER 14 Elders With Idols in Their Hearts — I YHWH Will Answer Him Myself — Even If Noakh, Daniyel, and Iyov Were There — The Four Severe Judgments

1 Then certain of the elders of Yisra'EL came to me and sat before me.

2 And the word of YHWH came to me:

3 Son of man, these men have taken their idols into their hearts, and set the stumbling block of their iniquity before their faces. Should I indeed let myself be consulted by them?

4 Therefore speak to them and say to them, Thus says the Creator YHWH: Any one of the house of Yisra'EL who takes his idols into his heart and sets the stumbling block of his iniquity before his face, and yet comes to the prophet, I YHWH will answer him myself because of the multitude of his idols,

5 That I may lay hold of the hearts of the house of Yisra'EL, who are all estranged from me through their idols.

6 Therefore say to the house of Yisra'EL, Thus says the Creator YHWH: Repent and turn away from your idols, and turn away your faces from all your abominations.

7 For any one of the house of Yisra'EL, or of the strangers who sojourn in Yisra'EL, who separates himself from me, taking his idols into his heart and putting the stumbling block of his iniquity before his face, and yet comes to a prophet to consult me through him, I YHWH will answer him myself.

8 And I will set my face against that man. I will make him a sign and a byword and cut him off from the midst of my people, and you shall know that I am YHWH.

9 And if the prophet is deceived and speaks a word, I YHWH have deceived that prophet, and I will stretch out my hand against him and will destroy him from the midst of my people Yisra'EL.

10 And they shall bear their punishment — the punishment of the prophet and the punishment of the inquirer shall be alike —

11 That the house of Yisra'EL may no more go astray from me, nor defile themselves anymore with all their transgressions, but that they may be my people and I may be their Creator, declares the Creator YHWH.

12 And the word of YHWH came to me:

13 Son of man, when a land sins against me by acting faithlessly, and I stretch out my hand against it and break its supply of bread and send famine upon it, and cut off from it man and beast,

14 Even if these three men, Noakh, Daniyel, and Iyov, were in it, they would deliver but their own lives by their righteousness, declares the Creator YHWH.

15 If I cause wild beasts to pass through the land, and they ravage it, and it be made desolate, so that no one may pass through because of the beasts,

16 Even if these three men were in it, as I live, declares the Creator YHWH, they would deliver neither sons nor daughters. They alone would be delivered, but the land would be desolate.

17 Or if I bring a sword upon that land and say, Let a sword pass through the land, and I cut off from it man and beast,

18 Though these three men were in it, as I live, declares the Creator YHWH, they would deliver neither sons nor daughters, but they alone would be delivered.

19 Or if I send a pestilence into that land and pour out my wrath upon it with blood, to cut off from it man and beast,

20 Even if Noakh, Daniyel, and Iyov were in it, as I live, declares the Creator YHWH, they would deliver neither son nor daughter. They would deliver but their own lives by their righteousness.

21 For thus says the Creator YHWH: How much more when I send upon Yerushalayim my four disastrous acts of judgment, sword, famine, wild beasts, and pestilence, to cut off from it man and beast.

22 But behold, there shall be left in it a remnant who will be brought out, both sons and daughters. Behold, when they come out to you, and you see their ways and their deeds, you will be consoled for the disaster that I have brought upon Yerushalayim, for all that I have brought upon it.

23 They will console you, when you see their ways and their deeds, and you shall know that I have not done without cause all that I have done in it, declares the Creator YHWH.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The DSS preserves Chapter 14 in close agreement with the Masoretic. The three righteous men — Noakh, Daniyel, and Iyov — are the covenant canon of individual righteousness cited against the false hope of intercession: even if the three most righteous figures known could intercede, they would only save themselves. The covenant judgment on Yerushalayim is beyond the reach of individual covenant righteousness to avert.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 14 in full. The Daniyel cited in verse 14 is the ancient figure of covenant wisdom known in the Ugaritic tradition — not the later Daniyel of the canonical book bearing that name. The Orit tradition preserves the distinction between the ancient Daniyel of covenant wisdom lore and the later exilic figure.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 14. Verse 4 — any man who takes his idols into his heart and yet comes to the prophet, I YHWH will answer him myself. The covenant principle of heart-idolatry: the idols in the heart — not merely the idols of stone and metal — are the covenant concern. A man can come to the covenant prophet with clean hands while harboring covenant idols in his heart. YHWH sees the heart, not the posture of inquiry.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 9 — if the prophet is deceived and speaks a word, I YHWH have deceived that prophet. The covenant paradox of prophetic deception: when the covenant community comes to a prophet with idols in their hearts seeking confirmation for what they already believe, YHWH allows the prophet to be deceived and to give the confirmation they seek — and then holds both the inquirer and the prophet accountable. Covenant inquiry with a predetermined conclusion is covenant manipulation.

YEHEZKEL — CHAPTER 15 The Allegory of the Vine Wood — What Is the Wood of the Vine — Good for Nothing — I Will Make the Land Desolate

1 And the word of YHWH came to me:

2 Son of man, how does the wood of the vine surpass any wood, the vine branch that is among the trees of the forest?

3 Is wood taken from it to make anything? Do people take a peg from it to hang any vessel on it?

4 Behold, it is given to the fire for fuel. When the fire has consumed both ends of it, and the middle of it is charred, is it useful for anything?

5 Behold, when it was whole, it was used for nothing. How much less, when the fire has consumed it and it is charred, can it ever be used for anything.

6 Therefore thus says the Creator YHWH: Like the wood of the vine among the trees of the forest, which I have given to the fire for fuel, so have I given up the inhabitants of Yerushalayim.

7 And I will set my face against them. Though they escape from the fire, the fire shall yet consume them, and you shall know that I am YHWH, when I set my face against them.

8 And I will make the land desolate, because they have acted faithlessly, declares the Creator YHWH.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The DSS preserves Chapter 15 in close agreement with the Masoretic. The vine allegory is the covenant reversal of the covenant community's self-identity: Yisra'EL had long used the vine as a covenant image of YHWH's people — planted, tended, productive. Here YHWH inverts the covenant vine metaphor: the wood of the vine is the most useless wood in the forest. Its only value is as fuel.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 15 in full. Verse 2-5 — the vine wood allegory establishes the logical covenant case before the covenant application: vine wood has no structural value, cannot be used for making things, cannot serve as a peg. Charred at both ends and in the middle, it is doubly useless. The covenant community has twice been burned — north and south — and the remnant is the charred middle.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 15. The brevity of Chapter 15 — only 8 verses — is the covenant rhetoric of the compressed judgment: the covenant case against Yerushalayim is so clear that it requires only the briefest covenant argument. Vine wood is useless. Yerushalayim has been useless as a covenant community. The covenant conclusion is inevitable.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 7 — though they escape from the fire, the fire shall yet consume them. The covenant inescapability of the judgment: those who survive the first wave of covenant judgment are not exempt from the next. The covenant fire that began with the first deportation has not finished. The remnant remaining in Yerushalayim are not the saved — they are the charred middle of the vine wood, awaiting the completion of the covenant burning.

THE GREAT ALLEGORIES — CHAPTERS 16 THROUGH 19

YEHEZKEL — CHAPTER 16 The Allegory of the Foundling Wife — Your Father Was an Amorite — I Spread My Garment Over You — You Played the Whore — Your Sisters Sodom and Samaria — I Will Remember My Covenant

1 Again the word of YHWH came to me:

2 Son of man, make known to Yerushalayim her abominations,

3 And say, Thus says the Creator YHWH to Yerushalayim: Your origin and your birth are of the land of the Kena'ani. Your father was an Amorite and your mother a Khittite.

4 And as for your birth, on the day you were born your cord was not cut, nor were you washed with water to cleanse you, nor rubbed with salt, nor wrapped in swaddling cloths.

5 No eye pitied you, to do any of these things to you out of compassion for you. But you were cast out on the open field, for you were abhorred, on the day that you were born.

6 And when I passed by you and saw you wallowing in your blood, I said to you in your blood, Live. I said to you in your blood, Live.

7 I made you flourish like a plant of the field. And you grew up and became tall and arrived at full adornment. Your breasts were formed, and your hair had grown, yet you were naked and bare.

8 When I passed by you again and saw you, behold, you were at the age for love, and I spread the corner of my garment over you and covered your nakedness. I made my vow to you and entered into a covenant with you, declares the Creator YHWH, and you became mine.

9 Then I bathed you with water and washed off your blood from you and anointed you with oil.

10 I clothed you also with embroidered cloth and shod you with fine leather. I wrapped you in fine linen and covered you with silk.

11 And I adorned you with ornaments and put bracelets on your wrists and a chain on your neck.

12 And I put a ring on your nose and earrings in your ears and a beautiful crown on your head.

13 Thus you were adorned with gold and silver, and your clothing was of fine linen and silk and embroidered cloth. You ate fine flour and honey and oil. You grew exceedingly beautiful and advanced to royalty.

14 And your renown went forth among the nations because of your beauty, for it was perfect through the splendor that I had bestowed on you, declares the Creator YHWH.

15 But you trusted in your beauty and played the whore because of your renown and lavished your whorings on any passerby — your beauty became his.

16 You took some of your garments and made for yourself colorful shrines, and on them played the whore. The like has never been, nor ever shall be.

17 You also took your beautiful jewels of my gold and of my silver, which I had given you, and made for yourself images of men, and with them played the whore.

18 And you took your embroidered garments to cover them, and set my oil and my incense before them.

19 Also my bread that I gave you — I fed you with fine flour and oil and honey — you set before them for a pleasing aroma. And so it was, declares the Creator YHWH.

20 And you took your sons and your daughters, whom you had borne to me, and these you sacrificed to them to be devoured. Were your whorings so small a matter

21 That you slaughtered my children and delivered them up as an offering by fire to them?

22 And in all your abominations and your whorings you did not remember the days of your youth, when you were naked and bare, wallowing in your blood.

23 And after all your wickedness — woe, woe to you, declares the Creator YHWH —

24 You built yourself a vaulted chamber and made yourself a lofty place in every square.

25 At the head of every street you built your lofty place and made your beauty an abomination, offering yourself to any passerby and multiplying your whorings.

26 You also played the whore with the Egyptians, your lustful neighbors, multiplying your whorings, to provoke me to anger.

27 Behold, therefore, I stretched out my hand against you and diminished your allotted portion and delivered you to the greed of your enemies, the daughters of the Pelishtim, who were ashamed of your lewd behavior.

28 You played the whore also with the Assyrians, because you were not satisfied. And even after that you were not satisfied.

29 You multiplied your whorings also with the trading land of Chaldea, and even with this you were not satisfied.

30 How sick is your heart, declares the Creator YHWH, because you did all these things, the deeds of a brazen prostitute,

31 Building your vaulted chamber at the head of every street, and making your lofty place in every square. Yet you were not like a prostitute, because you scorned payment.

32 Adulterous wife, who receives strangers instead of her husband.

33 Gifts are given to all prostitutes, but you gave your gifts to all your lovers, bribing them to come to you from every side with your whorings.

34 So you were different from other women in your whorings. No one solicited you to play the whore, and you gave payment, while no payment was given to you. Therefore you were different.

35 Therefore, O prostitute, hear the word of YHWH.

36 Thus says the Creator YHWH, Because your lust was poured out and your nakedness uncovered in your whorings with your lovers, and with all your abominable idols, and because of the blood of your children that you gave to them,

37 Therefore, behold, I will gather all your lovers with whom you took pleasure, all those you loved and all those you hated. I will gather them against you from every side and will uncover your nakedness to them, that they may see all your nakedness.

38 And I will judge you as women who commit adultery and shed blood are judged, and bring upon you the blood of wrath and jealousy.

39 And I will give you into their hands, and they shall throw down your vaulted chamber and break down your lofty places. They shall strip you of your clothes and take your beautiful jewels and leave you naked and bare.

40 They shall bring up a crowd against you, and they shall stone you and cut you to pieces with their swords.

41 And they shall burn your houses and execute judgments upon you in the sight of many women. I will make you stop playing the whore, and you shall also give payment no more.

42 So will I satisfy my wrath on you, and my jealousy shall depart from you. I will be calm and will no more be angry.

43 Because you have not remembered the days of your youth, but have enraged me with all these things, therefore, behold, I have returned your deeds upon your head, declares the Creator YHWH. Have you not committed lewdness in addition to all your abominations?

44 Behold, everyone who uses proverbs will use this proverb about you: Like mother, like daughter.

45 You are the daughter of your mother, who loathed her husband and her children. And you are the sister of your sisters, who loathed their husbands and their children. Your mother was a Khittite and your father an Amorite.

46 And your elder sister is Samaria, who lived with her daughters to the north of you. And your younger sister, who lived to the south of you, is Sodom with her daughters.

47 You not only walked in their ways and did according to their abominations. Within a very little time you were more corrupt than they in all your ways.

48 As I live, declares the Creator YHWH, your sister Sodom and her daughters have not done as you and your daughters have done.

49 Behold, this was the guilt of your sister Sodom: she and her daughters had pride, excess of food, and prosperous ease, but did not aid the poor and needy.

50 They were haughty and did an abomination before me. So I removed them, when I saw it.

51 Samaria has not committed half your sins. You have committed more abominations than they, and have made your sisters appear righteous by all the abominations that you have committed.

52 Bear your disgrace, you also, for you have intervened on behalf of your sisters. Because of your sins in which you acted more abominably than they, they are more in the right than you. So be ashamed, you also, and bear your disgrace, for you have made your sisters appear righteous.

53 I will restore their fortunes, both the fortunes of Sodom and her daughters, and the fortunes of Samaria and her daughters, and I will restore your own fortunes in their midst,

54 That you may bear your disgrace and be ashamed of all that you have done, becoming a consolation to them.

55 As for your sisters, Sodom and her daughters shall return to their former state, and Samaria and her daughters shall return to their former state, and you and your daughters shall return to your former state.

56 Was not your sister Sodom a byword in your mouth in the day of your pride,

57 Before your wickedness was uncovered? Now you have become an object of reproach for the daughters of Aram and all those around her, and for the daughters of the Pelishtim, those all around who despise you.

58 You bear the penalty of your lewdness and your abominations, declares YHWH.

59 For thus says the Creator YHWH: I will deal with you as you have done, you who have despised the oath in breaking the covenant,

60 Yet I will remember my covenant with you in the days of your youth, and I will establish for you an everlasting covenant.

61 Then you will remember your ways and be ashamed when you take your sisters, both your elder and your younger, and I give them to you as daughters, but not on account of the covenant with you.

62 I will establish my covenant with you, and you shall know that I am YHWH,

63 That you may remember and be confounded, and never open your mouth again because of your shame, when I atone for you for all that you have done, declares the Creator YHWH.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QEzekb preserves portions of Chapter 16. The foundling wife allegory is the most extensive and unflinching covenant marriage metaphor in the entire Hebrew prophetic corpus — 63 verses developing the covenant story of Yerushalayim from abandoned infant to covenant wife to covenant prostitute and back to covenant restoration. The DSS preserves the full arc of this covenant allegory.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 16 in full. Verse 3 — your father was an Amorite and your mother a Khittite. The covenant genealogical provocation: Yerushalayim's origin is not idealized as purely Israelite — it is the pre-covenant city of mixed Kena'anite heritage that YHWH adopted, not a people who came to him with covenant credentials. Covenant relationship began with YHWH's initiative, not Yerushalayim's covenant merit.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 16. Verse 49 — this was the guilt of your sister Sodom: pride, excess of food, prosperous ease, but she did not aid the poor and needy. The covenant redefinition of Sodom's sin: the tradition that has reduced Sodom's guilt to sexual violation is corrected by the covenant text itself. Sodom's primary covenant guilt is social covenant failure — abundance without covenant justice.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 60 — yet I will remember my covenant with you in the days of your youth, and I will establish for you an everlasting covenant. The covenant pivot within the longest covenant judgment speech in the book: after 59 verses of covenant indictment, YHWH declares covenant remembrance. The covenant is not broken by the covenant wife's infidelity — it is the covenant husband who remembers it when the covenant wife has forgotten everything.

YEHEZKEL — CHAPTER 17 The Two Eagles and the Vine — The Cedar Twig Transplanted — Tzidkiyahu's Broken Covenant — I Will Plant a Tender Twig on a High Mountain

1 The word of YHWH came to me:

2 Son of man, propound a riddle, and speak a parable to the house of Yisra'EL.

3 Say, Thus says the Creator YHWH: A great eagle with great wings and long pinions, rich in plumage of many colors, came to Lebanon and took the top of the cedar.

4 He broke off the topmost of its young twigs and carried it to a land of trade and set it in a city of merchants.

5 Then he took of the seed of the land and planted it in fertile soil. He placed it beside abundant waters. He set it like a willow twig.

6 And it sprouted and became a low spreading vine, and its branches turned toward him, and its roots remained where it stood. So it became a vine and produced branches and put out boughs.

7 And there was another great eagle with great wings and much plumage, and behold, this vine bent its roots toward him and shot forth its branches toward him from the bed where it was planted, that he might water it.

8 It had been planted on good soil by abundant waters, that it might produce branches and bear fruit and become a noble vine.

9 Say, Thus says the Creator YHWH: Will it thrive? Will he not pull up its roots and cut off its fruit, so that it withers — so that all its fresh sprouting leaves wither? It will not take a strong arm or many people to pull it from its roots.

10 Behold, it is planted. Will it thrive? Will it not utterly wither when the east wind strikes it — wither away on the bed where it sprouted?

11 Then the word of YHWH came to me:

12 Say now to the rebellious house, Do you not know what these things mean? Tell them, Behold, the king of Babylon came to Yerushalayim, and took her king and her princes and brought them to him to Babylon.

13 And he took one of the royal offspring and made a covenant with him, putting him under oath. The chief men of the land he had taken away,

14 That the kingdom might be humble and not lift itself up, and keep his covenant that it might stand.

15 But he rebelled against him by sending his ambassadors to Egypt, that they might give him horses and a large army. Will he thrive? Can one escape who does such things? Can he break the covenant and yet escape?

16 As I live, declares the Creator YHWH, surely in the place where the king dwells who made him king, whose oath he despised, and whose covenant with him he broke, in Babylon he shall die.

17 Pharaoh with his mighty army and great company will not help him in war, when mounds are cast up and siege walls built to cut off many lives.

18 He despised the oath in breaking the covenant, and behold, he gave his hand and did all these things. He shall not escape.

19 Therefore thus says the Creator YHWH: As I live, surely my oath that he despised, and my covenant that he broke, I will return upon his own head.

20 I will spread my net over him, and he shall be taken in my snare, and I will bring him to Babylon and enter into judgment with him there for the treachery he has committed against me.

21 And all the pick of his troops shall fall by the sword, and the survivors shall be scattered to every wind. And you shall know that I am YHWH who has spoken.

22 Thus says the Creator YHWH: I myself will take a sprig from the lofty top of the cedar and will set it out. I will break off from the topmost of its young twigs a tender one, and I myself will plant it on a high and lofty mountain.

23 On the mountain height of Yisra'EL will I plant it, that it may bear branches and produce fruit and become a noble cedar. And under it will dwell every kind of bird. In the shade of its branches birds of every sort will nest.

24 And all the trees of the field shall know that I am YHWH. I bring low the high tree, and make high the low tree. I dry up the green tree, and make the dry tree flourish. I am YHWH. I have spoken, and I will do it.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The DSS preserves Chapter 17 in close agreement with the Masoretic. The two eagles parable — Babylon and Egypt, between whom Tzidkiyahu vacillates — is the covenant political analysis of the final decade of Yehudah's monarchy compressed into a botanical riddle. The covenant treaty broken with Babylon is the same as the covenant oath sworn before YHWH — verse 19 — making Tzidkiyahu's political treachery a covenant violation against YHWH himself.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 17 in full. Verses 22-24 — the covenant restoration promise embedded at the close of the judgment parable: YHWH himself takes a tender twig from the cedar top and plants it on the high mountain of Yisra'EL. The same cedar imagery used for the Davidic dynasty's humiliation becomes the imagery of its covenant restoration — planted by YHWH himself on the covenant mountain.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 17. Verse 19 — my oath that he despised, and my covenant that he broke, I will return upon his own head. The covenant treaty made with Nevukhadnetzar was sworn in YHWH's name — making its violation a covenant offense against YHWH, not merely a political act of rebellion. Tzidkiyahu broke not just a political treaty but a covenant oath.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 24 — I bring low the high tree, and make high the low tree. I dry up the green tree, and make the dry tree flourish. The covenant reversal principle stated in its most compact form: YHWH's covenant activity is consistently the inversion of human assessments of status and vitality. The green and high are brought low; the dry and low are raised. The covenant logic applies to the Davidic dynasty: the stripped and transplanted twig becomes the noble cedar on the mountain.

YEHEZKEL — CHAPTER 18 The Soul That Sins Shall Die — Not the Son for the Father — If the Wicked Turns From His Sin — I Have No Pleasure in the Death of Anyone

1 The word of YHWH came to me:

2 What do you mean by repeating this proverb concerning the land of Yisra'EL, The fathers have eaten sour grapes, and the children's teeth are set on edge?

3 As I live, declares the Creator YHWH, this proverb shall no more be used by you in Yisra'EL.

4 Behold, all souls are mine. The soul of the father as well as the soul of the son is mine. The soul who sins shall die.

5 If a man is righteous and does what is just and right —

6 If he does not eat upon the mountains or lift up his eyes to the idols of the house of Yisra'EL, does not defile his neighbor's wife or approach a woman in her time of menstrual impurity,

7 Does not oppress anyone, but restores to the debtor his pledge, commits no robbery, gives his bread to the hungry and covers the naked with a garment,

8 Does not lend at interest or take any profit, withholds his hand from injustice, executes true justice between man and man,

9 Walks in my statutes, and keeps my rules by acting faithfully — he is righteous. He shall surely live, declares the Creator YHWH.

10 If he fathers a son who is violent, a shedder of blood, who does any of these things

11 — Though he himself did none of these things — who even eats upon the mountains, defiles his neighbor's wife,

12 Oppresses the poor and needy, commits robbery, does not restore the pledge, lifts up his eyes to the idols, commits abomination,

13 Lends at interest, and takes profit — shall he then live? He shall not live. He has done all these abominations. He shall surely die. His blood shall be upon himself.

14 Now suppose this man fathers a son who sees all the sins that his father has done. He sees, and does not do likewise.

15 He does not eat upon the mountains or lift up his eyes to the idols of the house of Yisra'EL, does not defile his neighbor's wife,

16 Does not oppress anyone, exacts no pledge, commits no robbery, but gives his bread to the hungry and covers the naked with a garment,

17 Withholds his hand from iniquity, takes no interest or profit, obeys my rules, and walks in my statutes — he shall not die for his father's iniquity. He shall surely live.

18 As for his father, because he practiced extortion, robbed his brother, and did what is not good among his people, behold, he shall die for his iniquity.

19 Yet you say, Why should not the son suffer for the iniquity of the father? When the son has done what is just and right, and has been careful to observe all my statutes, he shall surely live.

20 The soul who sins shall die. The son shall not suffer for the iniquity of the father, nor the father suffer for the iniquity of the son. The righteousness of the righteous shall be upon himself, and the wickedness of the wicked shall be upon himself.

21 But if a wicked person turns away from all his sins that he has committed and keeps all my statutes and does what is just and right, he shall surely live. He shall not die.

22 None of the transgressions that he has committed shall be remembered against him. For the righteousness that he has done he shall live.

23 Have I any pleasure in the death of the wicked, declares the Creator YHWH, and not rather that he should turn from his way and live?

24 But when a righteous person turns away from his righteousness and does injustice and does the same abominations that the wicked person does, shall he live? None of the righteous deeds that he has done shall be remembered. For the treachery of which he is guilty and the sin he has committed, for them he shall die.

25 Yet you say, The way of YHWH is not just. Hear now, O house of Yisra'EL: Is my way not just? Is it not your ways that are not just?

26 When a righteous person turns away from his righteousness and does injustice, he shall die for it. For the injustice that he has done he shall die.

27 Again, when a wicked person turns away from the wickedness he has committed and does what is just and right, he shall save his life.

28 Because he considered and turned away from all the transgressions that he had committed, he shall surely live. He shall not die.

29 Yet the house of Yisra'EL says, The way of YHWH is not just. O house of Yisra'EL, are my ways not just? Is it not your ways that are not just?

30 Therefore I will judge you, O house of Yisra'EL, every one according to his ways, declares the Creator YHWH. Repent and turn from all your transgressions, lest iniquity be your ruin.

31 Cast away from you all the transgressions that you have committed, and make yourselves a new heart and a new spirit. Why will you die, O house of Yisra'EL?

32 For I have no pleasure in the death of anyone, declares the Creator YHWH. So turn, and live.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The DSS preserves Chapter 18 in close agreement with the Masoretic. Chapter 18 is the covenant declaration of individual covenant accountability — the most direct counter to the collective punishment theology that the exiles had developed as a rationale for their situation. The covenant proverb of the sour grapes — fathers' sin visited on children — is abolished as a legitimate covenant theology.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 18 in full. Verse 23 — have I any pleasure in the death of the wicked, and not rather that he should turn from his way and live? — and verse 32 — I have no pleasure in the death of anyone, so turn and live — are the covenant declaration of YHWH's covenant desire: not covenant judgment but covenant repentance. The covenant judgment is not YHWH's preference but the covenant consequence of covenant refusal.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 18. Verse 31 — make yourselves a new heart and a new spirit. Here the covenant command to self-transformation — which Chapter 11:19 and Chapter 36:26 declare YHWH will perform — is commanded of the covenant community themselves. Both are simultaneously true in the covenant framework: YHWH will give the new heart, and the covenant community is commanded to pursue it.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 25 — yet you say, The way of YHWH is not just. The covenant community's protest against covenant individual accountability is the accusation that YHWH's covenant justice is inequitable. YHWH's covenant response is not defensive but direct: is my way not just? Is it not your ways that are not just? The covenant inversion: the community accusing YHWH of injustice is itself the covenant community that has been systematically unjust — Chapter 18:6-13.

YEHEZKEL — CHAPTER 19 A Lamentation for the Princes of Yisra'EL — The Lioness and Her Cubs — The Vine Transplanted to the Wilderness

1 And you, take up a lamentation for the princes of Yisra'EL,

2 And say: What was your mother? A lioness. Among lions she crouched. In the midst of young lions she reared her cubs.

3 And she brought up one of her cubs. He became a young lion. He learned to catch prey. He devoured men.

4 The nations heard about him. He was caught in their pit. And they brought him with hooks to the land of Egypt.

5 When she saw that she waited in vain, that her hope was lost, she took another of her cubs and made him a young lion.

6 He prowled among the lions. He became a young lion. And he learned to catch prey. He devoured men.

7 And he ravaged their strongholds and laid waste their cities. The land was appalled and all who were in it at the sound of his roaring.

8 Then the nations set against him from provinces on every side. They spread their net over him. He was taken in their pit.

9 With hooks they put him in a cage and brought him to the king of Babylon. They brought him into custody, that his voice should no more be heard on the mountains of Yisra'EL.

10 Your mother was like a vine in your vineyard, planted by the water. It was fruitful and full of branches by reason of abundant water.

11 Its strong stems became rulers' scepters. It towered aloft among the thick boughs. It was seen in its height with the mass of its branches.

12 But the vine was plucked up in fury, cast down to the ground. The east wind dried up its fruit. They were stripped off and withered. As for its strong stem, fire consumed it.

13 Now it is transplanted in the wilderness, in a dry and thirsty land.

14 And fire has gone out from the stem of its shoots, has consumed its fruit, so that there remains in it no strong stem, no scepter for ruling. This is a lamentation and has become a lamentation.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The DSS preserves Chapter 19 in close agreement with the Masoretic. The lamentation for the princes — in the qinah meter of Hebrew mourning poetry — uses both the lion and the vine as covenant metaphors for the Davidic dynasty. The two cubs are widely understood as Yeho'akhaz carried to Egypt — 2 Melakhim 23:33 — and Yeho'yakhin or Tzidkiyahu carried to Babylon.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 19 in full. Verse 14 — fire has gone out from the stem of its own shoots, has consumed its fruit. The covenant irony: the fire that destroys the vine comes from within the vine itself — from its own rulers. The covenant dynasty's destruction is self-inflicted through its own covenant violation, not merely externally imposed.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 19. Verse 14 — this is a lamentation and has become a lamentation. The double lamentation ending: it was written as a lament for what would happen, and it has now become the lament for what has happened. The prophetic future tense has collapsed into the covenant present tense of fulfilled judgment.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Chapter 19 is the covenant acknowledgment that the Davidic dynasty in its present form is finished. The lamentation form — qinah — is reserved for the dead and the irreversibly lost. YHWH does not speak here in the prophetic declaratory mode but in the mourning mode: the covenant prophet weeps over what covenant violation has produced. The covenant lamentation does not cancel the covenant restoration promises of Chapters 17:22-24 and 11:19-20 — it acknowledges the real and total loss that must be grieved before restoration can be received.

END OF PART TWO — CHAPTERS 12 THROUGH 19
